contentBlocks.original:
सावित्र्युवाच ।
तपसा धर्ममाराध्य पुष्करे भास्करः पुरा ।
धर्मं सूर्यःसुतं प्राप धर्मराजं नमाम्यहम् ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sāvitryuvācha ।
tapasā dharmamārādhya puṣkarē bhāskaraḥ purā ।
dharmaṃ sūryaḥsutaṃ prāpa dharmarājaṃ namāmyaham ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Sāvitrī said: Long ago, the Sun — through austerity (tapas), having worshipped Dharma at Puṣkara — obtained Dharma as his son, the Dharmarāja. I bow to that Dharmarāja.
contentBlocks.meanings: sāvitrī uvāca: Sāvitrī said | tapasā: through austerity | dharma-ārādhya: having worshipped Dharma | puṣkare: at Puṣkara (sacred lake) | bhāskaraḥ: the Sun | purā: formerly | dharmaṃ sūrya-sutaṃ prāpa: obtained Dharma as the son of the Sun | dharmarāja: the king of Dharma (Yama) | namāmi: I bow to

contentBlocks.original:
समता सर्वभूतेषु यस्य सर्वस्य साक्षिणः ।
अतो यन्नाम शमनमिति तं प्रणमाम्यहम् ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
samatā sarvabhūtēṣu yasya sarvasya sākṣiṇaḥ ।
atō yannāma śamanamiti taṃ praṇamāmyaham ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: He who is equanimous toward all beings, who is the witness of all — hence his name is Śamana (the pacifier/equalizer). I bow to that Yama, whose very name means Śamana (peace, equanimity).
contentBlocks.meanings: samatā: equanimity | sarva-bhūteṣu: toward all beings | yasya: whose | sarvasya sākṣiṇaḥ: who is the witness of all | ataḥ: hence | yan-nāma: whose name | śamana: the pacifier/one who brings peace (also: the one who ceases) | iti: called | praṇamāmi: I bow to | aham: I

contentBlocks.original:
येनान्तश्च कृतो विश्वे सर्वेषां जीविनां परम् ।
कामानुरूपं कालेन तं कृतान्तं नमाम्यहम् ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yēnāntaścha kṛtō viśvē sarvēṣāṃ jīvināṃ param ।
kāmānurūpaṃ kālēna taṃ kṛtāntaṃ namāmyaham ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: He who has made an end (anta) for all living beings in the world — according to their desires, in due time — that Kṛtānta (the one who brings the end determined by karma) I bow to.
contentBlocks.meanings: yena: by whom | antaḥ kṛtaḥ: end is made | viśve: in the world | sarveṣāṃ jīvinām: for all living beings | param: ultimate/final | kāma-anurūpam: according to their desires/karma | kālena: in due time | kṛtāntaṃ: Kṛtānta (the one who effects the end determined by karma) | namāmi: I bow to

contentBlocks.original:
बिभर्ति दण्डं दण्डाय पापिनां शुद्धिहेतवे ।
नमामि तं दण्डधरं यः शास्ता सर्वजीविनाम् ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bibharti daṇḍaṃ daṇḍāya pāpināṃ śuddhihētavē ।
namāmi taṃ daṇḍadharaṃ yaḥ śāstā sarvajīvinām ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: He who bears the staff (daṇḍa) as a punishment for sinners and for their purification — I bow to that Daṇḍadhara (staff-bearer), who is the ruler of all living beings.
contentBlocks.meanings: bibharti: bears | daṇḍa: staff/rod of punishment | daṇḍāya: as punishment | pāpinām: for sinners | śuddhi-hetave: for the purpose of purification | namāmi: I bow to | daṇḍa-dhara: staff-bearer (Yama's epithet) | yaḥ śāstā: who is the ruler | sarva-jīvinām: of all living beings

contentBlocks.original:
विश्वं च कलयत्येव यः सर्वेषु च सन्ततम् ।
अतीव दुर्निवार्यं च तं कालं प्रणमाम्यहम् ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
viśvaṃ cha kalayatyēva yaḥ sarvēṣu cha santatam ।
atīva durnivāryaṃ cha taṃ kālaṃ praṇamāmyaham ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: He who gathers and measures all beings continuously in everything — and whose [coming] is extremely difficult to prevent — I bow to that Kāla (Time/Death).
contentBlocks.meanings: viśvaṃ ca: all the world | kalayati: gathers/measures/counts | eva: indeed | yaḥ: who | sarveṣu: in all | ca santatam: continuously | atīva: extremely | durnivārya: difficult to prevent | kālaṃ: that Kāla (Time/Death) | praṇamāmi: I bow to

contentBlocks.original:
तपस्वी ब्रह्मनिष्ठो यः संयमी सञ्जितेन्द्रियः ।
जीवानां कर्मफलदस्तं यमं प्रणमाम्यहम् ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tapasvī brahmaniṣṭhō yaḥ saṃyamī sañjitēndriyaḥ ।
jīvānāṃ karmaphaladastaṃ yamaṃ praṇamāmyaham ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Who is an ascetic, established in Brahman, self-controlled, with conquered senses — who gives to living beings the fruits of their karma — I bow to that Yama.
contentBlocks.meanings: tapasvi: ascetic | brahma-niṣṭha: established in Brahman | saṃyamī: self-controlled | sañjita-indriya: with conquered senses | jīvānāṃ karma-phala-da: who gives to living beings the fruits of their karma | yamaṃ: Yama | praṇamāmi: I bow to

contentBlocks.original:
स्वात्मारामश्च सर्वज्ञो मित्रं पुण्यकृतां भवेत् ।
पापिनां क्लेशदो यस्तं पुण्यमित्रं नमाम्यहम् ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
svātmārāmaścha sarvajñō mitraṃ puṇyakṛtāṃ bhavēt ।
pāpināṃ klēśadō yastaṃ puṇyamitraṃ namāmyaham ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: He who is self-delighting (ātmārāma), all-knowing, a friend to those who have performed good deeds — who is a source of affliction to sinners — I bow to that Puṇyamitra (friend of the righteous).
contentBlocks.meanings: svātmārāma: self-delighting/established in the joy of the Self | sarva-jña: all-knowing | mitraṃ: friend | puṇya-kṛtām: to those who have performed good deeds | bhavet: is | pāpinām kleśa-da: source of affliction to sinners | puṇya-mitraṃ: friend of the righteous (Puṇyamitra) | namāmi: I bow to

contentBlocks.original:
यज्जन्म ब्रह्मणोंऽशेन ज्वलन्तं ब्रह्मतेजसा ।
यो ध्यायति परं ब्रह्म तमीशं प्रणमाम्यहम् ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yajjanma brahmaṇōṃ'śēna jvalantaṃ brahmatējasā ।
yō dhyāyati paraṃ brahma tamīśaṃ praṇamāmyaham ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: He whose birth is from a portion of Brahmā — blazing with Brahmic splendor — who meditates on the supreme Brahman — I bow to that Lord (Īśa).
contentBlocks.meanings: yaj-janma: whose birth | brahmaṇaḥ aṃśena: from a portion of Brahmā | jvalantaṃ: blazing | brahma-tejasā: with Brahmic splendor | yo dhyāyati: who meditates on | paraṃ brahma: the supreme Brahman | tam-īśaṃ: that Lord | praṇamāmi: I bow to

contentBlocks.original:
इत्युक्त्वा सा च सावित्री प्रणनाम यमं मुने ।
यमस्तां शक्तिभजनं कर्मपाकमुवाच ह ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ityuktvā sā cha sāvitrī praṇanāma yamaṃ munē ।
yamastāṃ śaktibhajanaṃ karmapākamuvācha ha ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Having said this, Sāvitrī bowed to Yama, O sage. Then Yama spoke to her — she who was devoted to power (śakti-bhajana) — about the ripening of karma.
contentBlocks.meanings: ity-uktvā: having said this | sā ca sāvitrī: she, Sāvitrī | praṇanāma: bowed to | yamam: Yama | mune: O sage (addressed to the narrator) | yamas tāṃ: Yama to her | śakti-bhajanaṃ: she who was devoted to power/śakti | karma-pākam: the ripening of karma | uvāca ha: said

contentBlocks.original:
इदं यमष्टकं नित्यं प्रातरुत्थाय यः पठेत् ।
यमात्तस्य भयं नास्ति सर्वपापात्प्रमुच्यते ॥ 10 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
idaṃ yamaṣṭakaṃ nityaṃ prātarutthāya yaḥ paṭhēt ।
yamāttasya bhayaṃ nāsti sarvapāpātpramuchyatē ॥ 10 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Whoever rises in the morning and recites this Yamaṣṭaka — has no fear from Yama, and is freed from all sins.
contentBlocks.meanings: idam: this | yamaṣṭakam: the Yama Aṣṭakam (eight verses on Yama) | nitya: daily | prātar-utthāya: having risen in the morning | yaḥ paṭhet: whoever recites | yamāt tasya bhayam nāsti: has no fear from Yama | sarva-pāpāt pramucyate: is freed from all sins

contentBlocks.original:
महापापी यदि पठेन्नित्यं भक्तिसमन्वितः ।
यमः करोति संशुद्धं कायव्यूहेन निश्चितम् ॥ 11 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mahāpāpī yadi paṭhēnnityaṃ bhaktisamanvitaḥ ।
yamaḥ karōti saṃśuddhaṃ kāyavyūhēna niśchitam ॥ 11 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Even a great sinner, if he recites this daily with devotion — Yama surely makes him completely purified through the cycle of bodies (kāya-vyūha).
contentBlocks.meanings: mahā-pāpī: great sinner | yadi paṭhet: if he recites | nitya: daily | bhakti-samanvitaḥ: endowed with devotion | yamaḥ karoti: Yama makes | saṃśuddha: completely purified | kāya-vyūhena: through the cycle/arrangement of bodies | niścitam: certainly

contentBlocks.translation: Thus [ends] the thirty-first chapter of the ninth skandha of the great Purāṇa — the Śrī Devī Bhāgavata Mahāpurāṇa.
contentBlocks.meanings: iti: thus | śrī-mat-devī-bhāgavate mahā-purāṇe: in the great Purāṇa — the blessed Devī Bhāgavata | nava-ma-skandhe: in the ninth skandha (book) | eka-triṃśa-adhyāyaḥ: the thirty-first chapter
